actionable_feedback: Open and attach the full operations_memo.md (not just the preview). Confirm the memo contains the plain-Python calculation steps used to compute the fit (explicit normal-equation matrices or the full least-squares sum-of-squares calculations) so an auditor can re-run exactly.

Add an explicit, single-line tie-break policy for residual == tolerance (e.g., "residual_pct >= tolerance_pct => needs_review" or "residual_pct > tolerance_pct => needs_review") and record that rule in the memo and CSV generation code.

Document the plain-Python code or arithmetic used to compute the linear fit (including the derived A^T A and A^T y, or the explicit sums) and include the raw numeric intermediate values so independent reproducers can verify the coefficients exactly.

Explain the operational interpretation of the negative slope in the memo (whether the model is diagnostic only, whether sign indicates model misspecification, and recommended next operational action such as inverting sign or treating fit as a red flag).
Reconcile the semantic_score discrepancy in the grader/raw outputs: either remove/clarify semantic_score in model outputs or add the missing derivation so semantic grading is truly reproducible.

Add a short verification section in the memo listing the exact numeric assertions in the CSV (predicted values, residuals, residual_pct) and a one-line reproduction command (e.g., a plain-Python script invocation) that outputs identical numbers.

feedback_summary: Artifacts satisfy the artifact-contract checks and the numeric examples in the CSV match the memo's stated fit. The deliverables are reproducible from the embedded packet using plain-Python arithmetic. Minor issues remain: operations_memo.md preview is truncated (full-file not reviewed), and there is an inconsistency in raw/model outputs that record semantic_score=1.0 while deterministic precheck & verifier note semantic correctness was not fully evaluated. The grader/verifier recommend adding explicit numeric derivation (normal-equation or sum-of-squares) and tie-break rules for edge cases (e.g., residual == tolerance).

extra_model_fields.notes: No external data or hidden references were used. The environment lacked numpy and the worker switched to dependency-free arithmetic - include that explicit note in the memo so auditors understand reproducibility constraints.
primary_issues: semantic_score inconsistency across model/deterministic outputs; derivation steps missing in memo for independent semantic verification.
no explicit tie-break rule documented for residual == tolerance.

Include the plain-Python script or code block that produced the fit and the exact command to run it in the workspace; run the script in a clean environment to confirm numeric reproducibility.
Document tie-break logic for residual_pct == tolerance_pct and add one-line statement in the memo that the CSV uses this rule.
